name: Wholesale Distributor Customer Price Sheet Generator
one_liner: Automates per-customer PDF price sheet generation and email distribution for regional wholesale distributors
monthly_costs: PDFmonkey €5–€15/month; n8n VPS $5/month; Airtable free tier; Google Sheets free; Gmail via Google Workspace $12/month; total: ~$22–$32/month

node_graph.role: Receives POST from operator or external cron when master price list is updated; payload carries optional run_date and client_filter parameters
Reads master price list sheet (columns: item_id, description, unit, cost, category) from operator-managed Google Sheet
Reads customer account table (columns: account_name, tier_multiplier, primary_contact_email, logo_url, active flag) filtered to active=true
Iterates one customer account at a time to prevent PDFmonkey rate-limit collisions; batch size 1

Applies each account's tier_multiplier to every line item from the master sheet; formats result as JSON payload matching the per-client PDFmonkey Handlebars template schema (logo_url, account_name, effective_date, line_items array)
POSTs JSON payload to PDFmonkey API /v1/documents with the pre-configured per-client template_id; polls returned document URL until status=done
Emails rendered PDF as attachment to account primary_contact_email with subject 'Your [Distributor Name] Price Sheet — Effective [date]'
Logs send record: account_id, send_timestamp, pdf_url, version_hash for audit trail and idempotency on re-runs

external_credentials: Google Sheets OAuth2 (connected to master price list spreadsheet)
Airtable API key (customer account database base)
PDFmonkey API key (account with pre-built Handlebars template per distributor client)
Gmail OAuth2 via Google Workspace account (outbound price sheet distribution)
n8n_workflow_spec.expected_runtime: 45–90 seconds for a 50-account batch (sequential PDF generation + email dispatch at ~1–1.5 seconds per account)
n8n_workflow_spec.frequency: On demand via webhook; production target is weekly Monday 6 AM triggered by free external cron (cron-job.org) hitting the webhook URL
out_of_scope: Customer self-service portal where account contacts can view price history or download previous sheets on demand
Automated master price list ingestion from supplier EDI feeds, ERP exports, or emailed supplier PDFs
Delivery failure tracking, bounce handling, or read-receipt confirmation for undelivered price sheet emails
n8n_workflow_spec.success_metric: Within 90 minutes of webhook trigger, every active account in the Airtable customer table has a corresponding log entry with a non-null send_timestamp, and a spot-check of 3 account contact inboxes shows received emails containing a PDF with item count matching the master sheet row count and prices equal to master cost × that account's tier_multiplier
n8n_workflow_spec.risky_assumption: Regional wholesale distributors ($5M–$50M revenue) will pay $400–$600/month for automated price sheet distribution rather than spending 3 hours having an inside-sales rep do it manually
